Start with the concept, the datasheet, the lecture, the diagram, or the question. I try to understand the vocabulary first, because confusion often begins with vague words.
Turn the idea into something concrete. A small module, a model, a note, a testbench, a tool, or a rough prototype is better than perfect intention.
Let the system meet reality. Inputs become messy, assumptions become visible, and the design starts telling the truth.
Slow down and isolate the failure. Trace the signal, check the timing, reread the warning, and separate what I know from what I am guessing.
Write down what happened while the lesson is still warm. The mistake matters because it can become a map.
Explain it in plain language. If I cannot explain the design, I probably do not understand the design yet.
Return with a sharper model. The next build starts with the previous mistake already converted into structure.
